metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students develop advanced metalworking skills including welding, cutting, and fabrication techniques. This course explores metallurgy, design for fabrication, and creation of metal structures and objects with emphasis on safety and craftsmanship.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in welding, metal fabrication, metallurgy, and advanced metalworking techniques.

big_ideas.statement: Metalworking transforms raw metals through cutting, shaping, joining, and heat treatment to create functional and artistic objects.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding material properties and processing techniques enables effective metalworking and creation of quality metal products.

big_ideas.statement: Welding is a critical joining method that, when executed properly, creates strong, reliable connections in metal structures.
big_ideas.elaboration: Proficiency in welding techniques, metallurgy, and quality control ensures creation of safe, durable welded assemblies.

big_ideas.statement: Design for fabrication requires understanding metalworking processes and constraints to create designs that are practical and economical.
big_ideas.elaboration: Designers must consider material limitations, process capabilities, and manufacturing costs to create feasible metalwork designs.

big_ideas.statement: Safety in metalworking requires strict adherence to procedures, proper equipment use, and hazard awareness.
big_ideas.elaboration: Safety practices protect metalworkers and ensure quality of products and safety of end-users.

competencies.raw: Design metal objects considering fabrication processes
i_can_statements: I can develop designs for metal fabrication
I can consider metalworking process capabilities
I can design for welding and joining methods
I can design with material and cost constraints

competencies.raw: Plan metal fabrication sequences and procedures
i_can_statements: I can plan cutting and forming sequences
I can design welding sequences for quality
I can plan heat treatment procedures
I can develop fabrication work instructions

competencies.raw: Design metal structures that are safe and efficient
i_can_statements: I can analyze loads and stresses in metal structures
I can select appropriate materials and sizes
I can design joints and connections
I can ensure structural adequacy

competencies.raw: Evaluate metalwork quality and recommend improvements
i_can_statements: I can assess fabrication quality
I can identify defects and failures
I can analyze metalworking techniques
I can recommend process improvements
I can execute MIG/MAG welding
I can execute TIG welding
I can perform stick (SMAW) welding
I can select appropriate welding processes

competencies.raw: Cut and shape metal using various techniques
i_can_statements: I can use oxyacetylene cutting equipment
I can operate plasma cutting equipment
I can use grinding and finishing tools
I can perform bending and forming operations

competencies.raw: Apply heat treatment and finishing techniques
i_can_statements: I can perform heat treatment procedures
I can anneal, harden, and temper metals
I can perform surface finishing
I can paint and coat metal surfaces
I can assemble and tack components
I can perform quality control checks
I can create fit-up and alignment
I can fabricate to specifications

competencies.raw: Operate welding equipment safely and effectively
i_can_statements: I can set up and operate MIG welders
I can set up and operate TIG welders
I can maintain welding equipment
I can troubleshoot equipment problems

competencies.raw: Use metal cutting and forming equipment
i_can_statements: I can operate oxyacetylene cutting equipment
I can operate plasma cutting systems
I can use power tools and grinders
I can operate machines safely

competencies.raw: Apply metallurgy knowledge to welding and fabrication
i_can_statements: I can understand metal properties and behaviour
I can select appropriate welding processes for metals
I can prevent defects through process control
I can understand heat effects on metal
I can assess robotic welding systems
I can understand advanced welding processes
I can evaluate new materials and alloys
I can identify technology innovations

areas.name: Welding Processes and Techniques
topics: Arc welding: MIG/MAG, TIG, and stick (SMAW)
Welding technique: travel speed, heat input, deposition

areas.name: Metallurgy and Material Properties
topics: Iron and steel composition and properties

areas.name: Safety and Professional Metalworking
topics: Safe handling of materials and equipment
